fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Landscaped Area; Pedestrian Walkways
fr.recommendations: Ensuring that pedestrian walkways are clearly marked and unobstructed could facilitate emergency responder movement and navigation throughout the property. Maintaining landscaped areas to prevent overgrowth near building access points may enhance visibility and accessibility, allowing responders to quickly identify and reach necessary locations. Evaluating the roof system for any features that might impact aerial access or visibility from above could support efficient emergency operations. Keeping pathways leading to the main building well-maintained and free of potential obstructions may assist in timely responder access and staging, which would generally improve the overall effectiveness of emergency response efforts on site.

wildfire.conditions: Scattered Trees; Open Green Space; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetated Perimeter; Open Terrain; Defensible Space
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space by maintaining existing open areas and managing vegetation adjacent to the structure could reduce fuel continuity and improve property preparedness. It would be beneficial to ensure separation between scattered trees and the structure, potentially increasing vegetation management efforts in these areas to limit combustible material. Preserving open green spaces and maintaining open terrain around the property would support long-term wildfire resilience and provide effective fuel breaks. Managing the vegetated perimeter with adequate separation could further reduce the possibility of fire reaching the vicinity of the structure. Ongoing vegetation management efforts near access points and open areas would aid in supporting firefighter access and improving suppression operations when needed.
